name: openap

metadata.name: openap
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3)
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Open Aircraft Performance Model (OpenAP) in Python

documentation:
# OpenAP: Open Aircraft Performance Model and Toolkit

This repository contains the OpenAP model data and Python packages for aircraft performance and emission calculations.

## 🕮 User Guide

The OpenAP handbook is available at [openap.dev](https://openap.dev/).

## Installation

Install the latest stable release from PyPI:

```sh
pip install --upgrade openap
```

Install the development branch from GitHub (may not be stable):

```sh
pip install --upgrade git+https://github.com/junzis/openap
```

## Content

### Model Data

Data in this repository includes:

- Aircraft data: Collected from open literature.
- Engine data: Primarily from the ICAO emission data-bank, including fuel flow and emissions.
- Drag polar model data: Exclusively derived from open data ([reference](https://research.tudelft.nl/files/71038050/published_OpenAP_drag_polar.pdf)).
- Fuel model data: Polynomial models derived from the [acropole model](https://github.com/DGAC/Acropole) by [@JarryGabriel](https://github.com/JarryGabriel).
- Kinematic data: The kinematic model describes speed, altitude, and vertical rate ([reference](https://github.com/junzis/wrap)).
- Navigation data: Airport and waypoints obtained from [X-Plane](https://developer.x-plane.com/docs/data-development-documentation/).

### Python Packages

The OpenAP Python library includes the following packages:

- `prop`: Module for accessing aircraft and engine properties.
- `aero`: Module for common aeronautical conversions.
- `nav`: Module for accessing navigation information.
- `thrust`: Module provides `Thrust()` class for computing aircraft thrust.
- `drag`: Module provides `Drag()` class for computing aircraft drag.
- `fuel`: Module provides `FuelFlow()` class for computing fuel consumption.
- `emission`: Module provides `Emission()` class for computing aircraft emissions.
- `kinematic`: Module provides `WRAP()` class for accessing kinematic performance data.
- `phase`: Module provides `FlightPhase()` class for determining flight phases.
- `gen`: Module provides `FlightGenerator()` class for trajectory generation.

Examples:

```python
import openap

openap.prop.aircraft("A320")
fuelflow = openap.FuelFlow("A320")
fuelflow.enroute(mass, tas, alt) # -> kg/s
```

The input parameters can be scalar, list, or ndarray. Most of the OpenAP methods' parameters are in aeronautical units, such as knots, feet, feet/min. The mass is always in SI units, i.e., kilograms.

### Add-ons

The OpenAP library can also be used to interact with BADA performance models if you have access to the BADA data from EUROCONTROL. You can use the following code:

```python
from openap.addon import bada4

fuelflow = bada4.FuelFlow()
```

The methods and attributes of `openap.addon.bada4.FuelFlow()` are the same as those of `openap.FuelFlow()`.

## Symbolic Implementation for CasADi

The OpenAP model can also be used with the CasADi library for symbolic computations. The symbolic model is available in the `openap.casadi` package. For example, you can use the following code to create a symbolic model for fuel flow:

```python
from openap.casadi import FuelFlow

fuelflow = FuelFlow()
```

All the methods of `openap.casadi.FuelFlow()` are the same as those of `openap.FuelFlow()`, and they are now symbolic functions that can be used to compute fuel flow for given flight conditions in CasADi `DM`, `SX`, or `MX` types.

How did we implement this? When the `casadi` module is initiated, a metaclass is used to replace the `sci` function from `numpy`, which overrides all the `numpy` functions with `casadi` functions. For more details, check the `openap/casadi/__init__.py` code.

## Citing OpenAP

```
@article{sun2020openap,
  title={OpenAP: An open-source aircraft performance model for air transportation studies and simulations},
  author={Sun, Junzi and Hoekstra, Jacco M and Ellerbroek, Joost},
  journal={Aerospace},
  volume={7},
  number={8},
  pages={104},
  year={2020},
  publisher={Multidisciplinary Digital Publishing Institute}
}
```

modules.name: openap.thrust
documentation:
OpenAP thrust model.

Simplified two-shaft turbofan model based on:

- M. Bartel, T. M. Young, Simplified Thrust and Fuel Consumption
Models for Modern Two-Shaft Turbofan Engines

- C. Svoboda, Turbofan engine database as a preliminary design (cruise thrust)

classes.name: openap.thrust.Thrust

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Thrust object.

Args:
    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).
    eng (string): Engine type (for example: CFM56-5A3).

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust._dfunc
documentation:
Linear fit to the data from Table 2 in Bartel and Young (2008).

Args:
    mratio (float or ndarray): Ratio of mach number to reference mach
        number

Returns:
    float or ndarray: parameter 'd' in Equation 15 from Bartel
        and Young (2008)

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust._nfunc
documentation:
Linear fit to data from Table 3 in Bartel and Young (2008),
assuming the following climb rates:

Fast climb : 4000 ft / min
Moderate climb : 2500 ft / min
Slow climb : 1000 ft / min

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust._mfunc
documentation: Based on data from Table 4 in Bartel and Young (2008).

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust.takeoff
documentation:
Calculate thrust at take-off condition.

Args:
    tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (kt).
    alt (float or ndarray): Altitude of the runway (ft). Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Total thrust (unit: N).

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust.cruise
documentation:
Calculate thrust at the cruise.

Args:
    tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (kt).
    alt (float or ndarray): Altitude (ft).

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Total thrust (unit: N).

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust.climb
documentation:
Calculate thrust during the climb.

Args:
    tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (kt).
    alt (float or ndarray): Altitude (ft)
    roc (float or ndarray): Vertical rate (ft/min).

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Total thrust (unit: N).

methods.name: openap.thrust.Thrust.descent_idle
documentation:
Idle thrust during the descent.

Note: The idle thrust at the descent is taken as 7% of the maximum
available thrust. This may (likely) differ from actual idle thrust.

Args:
    tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (kt).
    alt (float or ndarray): Altitude (ft)

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Total thrust (unit: N).
Simplified two-shaft turbofan model.

modules.name: openap.drag
documentation: OpenAP drag model.

classes.name: openap.drag.Drag

methods.name: openap.drag.Drag.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Drag object.

Args:
    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).
    wave_drag (bool): enable Wave drag model (experimental).

methods.name: openap.drag.Drag.load_drag_model
documentation:
Find and construct the drag polar model.

Returns:
    dict: drag polar model parameters.

methods.name: openap.drag.Drag.clean
documentation:
Compute drag at clean configuration (considering compressibility).

Args:
    mass (int or ndarray): Mass of the aircraft (unit: kg).
    tas (int or ndarray): True airspeed (unit: kt).
    alt (int or ndarray): Altitude (unit: ft).
    vs (float or ndarray): Vertical rate (unit: feet/min). Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    int: Total drag (unit: N).

methods.name: openap.drag.Drag.nonclean
documentation:
Compute drag at at non-clean configuration.

Args:
    mass (int or ndarray): Mass of the aircraft (unit: kg).
    tas (int or ndarray): True airspeed (unit: kt).
    alt (int or ndarray): Altitude (unit: ft).
    flap_angle (int or ndarray): flap deflection angle (unit: degree).
    vs (float or ndarray): Vertical rate (unit: feet/min). Defaults to 0.
    landing_gear (bool): Is landing gear extended? Defaults to False.

Returns:
    int or ndarray: Total drag (unit: N).
Compute the drag of an aircraft.

variables.name: openap.contrail.propulsion_efficiency

modules.name: openap.gen
documentation:
Trajectory generator based on WRAP model.

This module defines the Generator class than can be used to generate flight
trajectories using the WRAP kinematic performance model from OpenAP.

Examples:

```
trajgen = Generator(ac='a320')

trajgen.enable_noise()   # enable Gaussian noise in trajectory data

df_cl = trajgen.climb(dt=10, cas_const_cl=280, mach_const_cl=0.78, alt_cr=35000)
df_cl = trajgen.climb(dt=10, random=True)  # using radomparameters

df_de = trajgen.descent(dt=10, cas_const_de=280, mach_const_de=0.78, alt_cr=35000)
df_de = trajgen.descent(dt=10, random=True)

df_cr = trajgen.cruise(dt=60, range_cr=2000, alt_cr=35000, m_cr=0.78)
df_cr = trajgen.cruise(dt=60, random=True)

df_all = trajgen.complete(dt=10, alt_cr=35000, m_cr=0.78,
                            cas_const_cl=280, mach_const_cl=0.78,
                            cas_const_de=280, mach_const_de=0.78)
df_all = trajgen.complete(dt=10, random=True)
```

classes.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator

methods.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator.__init__
documentation:
Intitialize the generator.

Args:
    ac (str): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).
    random_seed (int): Random seed for noise generation.

methods.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator.enable_noise
documentation:
Adding noise to the generated trajectory.

The noise model is based on ADS-B Version 1&2, NACv=3 and NACp=10

methods.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator.climb
documentation:
Generate climb trajectory based on WRAP model.

Args:
    **dt (int): Time step in seconds.
    **cas_const_cl (int): Constant CAS for climb (kt).
    **mach_const_cl (float): Constant Mach for climb (-).
    **alt_cr (int): Target cruise altitude (ft).
    **random (bool): Generate trajectory with random parameters.

methods.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator.descent
documentation:
Generate descent trajectory based on WRAP model.

Args:
    **dt (int): Time step in seconds.
    **cas_const_de (int): Constant CAS for climb (kt).
    **mach_const_de (float): Constant Mach for climb (-).
    **alt_cr (int): top of descent altitude (ft).
    **random (bool): Generate trajectory with random parameters, default to False.
    **withcr (bool): Include a short cruise segment of 60 seconds, default to True.

methods.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator.cruise
documentation:
Generate descent trajectory based on WRAP model.

Args:
    **dt (int): Time step in seconds.
    **range_cr (int): Cruise range (km).
    **alt_cr (int): Cruise altitude (ft).
    **mach_cr (float): Cruise Mach number (-).
    **random (bool): Generate trajectory with random parameters.

methods.name: openap.gen.FlightGenerator.complete
documentation:
Generate a complete trajectory based on WRAP model.

Args:
    **dt (int): Time step in seconds.
    **cas_const_cl (int): Constant CAS for climb (kt).
    **mach_const_cl (float): Constant Mach for climb (-).
    **cas_const_de (int): Constant CAS for climb (kt).
    **mach_const_de (float): Constant Mach for climb (-).
    **range_cr (int): Cruise range (km).
    **alt_cr (int): Target cruise altitude (ft).
    **mach_cr (float): Cruise Mach number (-).
    **random (bool): Generate trajectory with random parameters.
Generate trajectory using WRAP model.

modules.name: openap.casadi.aero_override
documentation: aero.py adapted for CasADi

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.atmos
documentation:
Compute press, density and temperature at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    (SX, SX, SX) or (MX, MX, MX):
        Air pressure (Pa), density (kg/m3), and temperature (K).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.temperature
documentation:
Compute air temperature at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Air temperature (K).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.pressure
documentation:
Compute air pressure at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Air pressure (Pa).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.density
documentation:
Compute air density at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Air density (kg/m3).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.vsound
documentation:
Compute speed of sound at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: speed of sound (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.distance
documentation:
Compute distance between two (or two series) of coordinates using Harversine formula.

Args:
    lat1 (SX or MX): Starting latitude (in degrees).
    lon1 (SX or MX): Starting longitude (in degrees).
    lat2 (SX or MX): Ending latitude (in degrees).
    lon2 (SX or MX): Ending longitude (in degrees).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters). Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Distance (in meters).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.bearing
documentation:
Compute the bearing between two (or two series) of coordinates.

Args:
    lat1 (SX or MX): Starting latitude (in degrees).
    lon1 (SX or MX): Starting longitude (in degrees).
    lat2 (SX or MX): Ending latitude (in degrees).
    lon2 (SX or MX): Ending longitude (in degrees).

Returns:
    SX or MX: Bearing (in degrees). Between 0 and 360.

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.h_isa
documentation:
Compute ISA altitude for a given pressure.

Args:
    p (SX or MX): Pressure (in Pa).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: altitude (m).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.latlon
documentation:
Get lat/lon given current point, distance and bearing.

Args:
    lat1 (SX or MX): Starting latitude (in degrees).
    lon1 (SX or MX): Starting longitude (in degrees).
    d (SX or MX): distance from point 1 (meters)
    brg (SX or MX): bearing at point 1 (in degrees)
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (in meters). Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    lat2: Point latitude.
    lon2: Point longitude

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.tas2mach
documentation:
Convert true airspeed to mach number at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_tas (SX or MX): True airspeed (m/s).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: mach number.

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.mach2tas
documentation:
Convert mach number to true airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    mach (SX or MX): Mach number.
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: True airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.eas2tas
documentation:
Convert equivalent airspeed to true airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_eas (SX or MX): Equivalent airspeed (m/s).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: True airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.tas2eas
documentation:
Convert true airspeed to equivalent airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_tas (SX or MX): True airspeed (m/s).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Equivalent airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.cas2tas
documentation:
Convert calibrated airspeed to true airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_cas (SX or MX): Equivalent airspeed (m/s).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: True airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.tas2cas
documentation:
Convert true airspeed to calibrated airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_tas (SX or MX): True airspeed (m/s).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Calibrated airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.mach2cas
documentation:
Convert mach number to calibrated airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    mach (SX or MX): Mach number.
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Calibrated airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.cas2mach
documentation:
Convert calibrated airspeed to mach number  at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_cas (SX or MX): Calibrated airspeed (m/s).
    h (SX or MX): Altitude (m).
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Mach number.

functions.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.crossover_alt
documentation:
Convert the crossover altitude given constant CAS and Mach.

Args:
    v_cas (SX or MX): Calibrated airspeed (m/s).
    mach (SX or MX): Mach number.
    dT (SX or MX): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    SX or MX: Altitude (m).

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.kts

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.ft
documentation: ft -> m

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.fpm
documentation: ft/min -> m/s

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.inch
documentation: inch -> m

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.sqft
documentation: 1 square foot

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.nm
documentation: nautical mile -> m

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.lbs
documentation: pound -> kg

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.g0
documentation: m/s2, Sea level gravity constant

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.R
documentation: m2/(s2 x K), gas constant, sea level ISA

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.p0
documentation: Pa, air pressure, sea level ISA

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.rho0
documentation: kg/m3, air density, sea level ISA

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.T0
documentation: K, temperature, sea level ISA

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.gamma
documentation: cp/cv for air

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.gamma1
documentation: (gamma-1)/2 for air

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.gamma2
documentation: gamma/(gamma-1) for air

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.beta
documentation: [K/m] ISA temp gradient below tropopause

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.r_earth
documentation: m, average earth radius

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.a0
documentation: m/s, sea level speed of sound ISA, sqrt(gamma*R*T0)

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.deg
documentation: radians -> degrees

variables.name: openap.casadi.aero_override.rad
documentation: degrees -> radians

modules.name: openap.phase
documentation: Using fuzzy logic to indentify flight phase in trajectory data.

classes.name: openap.phase.FlightPhase

documentation: Initialize of the FlightPhase object.

methods.name: openap.phase.FlightPhase.set_trajectory
documentation:
Set trajectory data.

Args:
    ts (list): Time (unit: second).
    alt (list): Altitude (unit: ft).
    spd (list): True airspeed (unit: kt).
    roc (list): Rate of climb (unit: ft/min). Negative for descent.

methods.name: openap.phase.FlightPhase.phaselabel
documentation:
Fuzzy logic for determining phase label.

Args:
    twindow (int): Time window in number of seconds. Default to 60.

Returns:
    list: Labels could be: ground [GND], climb [CL], descent [DE],
        cruise [CR], leveling [LVL].

methods.name: openap.phase.FlightPhase.plot_logics
documentation: Visualize fuzzy logic membership functions.

methods.name: openap.phase.FlightPhase.flight_phase_indices
documentation:
Get the indices of data, of which different flight phase start.

Returns:
    dict: Indices for takeoff (TO), initial climb (IC), climb (CL),
        cruise (CR), descent (DE), final approach (FA), landing (LD).
Fuzzy logic flight phase identification.

methods.name: openap.emission.Emission.sox

Compute SOx emission with given fuel flow.

Args:

methods.name: openap.emission.Emission.nox

Compute NOx emission with given fuel flow, speed, and altitude.

Args:

methods.name: openap.emission.Emission.co

Compute CO emission with given fuel flow, speed, and altitude.

Returns:
    float: CO emission from all engines (unit: g/s).

methods.name: openap.emission.Emission.hc
documentation:
Compute HC emission with given fuel flow, speed, and altitude.

Args:
    ffac (float or ndarray): Fuel flow for all engines (unit: kg/s).
    tas (float or ndarray): Speed (unit: kt).
    alt (int or ndarray): Aircraft altitude (unit: ft).

Returns:
    float: HC emission from all engines (unit: g/s).
Emission model based on ICAO emmision databank.

modules.name: openap.kinematic
documentation:
OpenAP kinematic model.

This module implements functions to access the WRAP kinematic model.

Examples:

: WRAP model can be accessed as follows:

  ```
  from openap import WRAP
  wrap = WRAP(ac='A320')
  wrap.cruise_mach()
  ```

  with result:

  ```
  {
      'default': 0.78,
      'minimum': 0.75,
      'maximum': 0.8,
      'statmodel': 'beta',
      'statmodel_params': [17.82, 5.05, 0.62, 0.2]
  }
  ```

classes.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.__init__
documentation:
Initialize WRAP object.

Args:
    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).

documentation: Get takeoff speed.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.takeoff_distance
documentation: Get takeoff takeoff distance.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.takeoff_acceleration
documentation: Get takeoff takeoff acceleration.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.initclimb_vcas
documentation: Get initial climb CAS.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.initclimb_vs
documentation: Get initial climb vertical rate.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.climb_range
documentation: Get climb range distance (in km).

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.climb_const_vcas
documentation: Get speed for constant CAS climb.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.climb_const_mach
documentation: Get speed during constant Mach climb.

methods.name: openap.kinematic.WRAP.climb_cross_alt_concas
documentation: Get cross over altitude when constant CAS climb starts.

documentation: Compute the drag coefficient (CD)

methods.name: openap.addon.bada4.Drag.clean
documentation:
Compute drag at clean configuration.

Args:
    mass (float | ndarray): Mass of the aircraft (kg).
    tas (float | ndarray): True airspeed (kt).
    alt (float | ndarray): Altitude (ft).
    vs (float): Vertical rate (feet/min). Default: 0.

Returns:
    float | ndarray: Total drag (N).

Compute the drag of an aircraft using BADA4 models.

Attributes:
    ac (str): aircraft type (e.g.: A320 or A320-231).
    scalar (float): Scalar value for drag coefficient calculation.
    d_ (ndarray): Array of drag coefficient parameters.
    mach_max (float): Maximum Mach number.
    S (float): Wing surface area (m^2).

Methods:
    _cd_base(cl, mach):
        Compute the base drag coefficient (CD) for givenlift coefficient (CL)
        and Mach number.

    _cd(cl, mach):
        Compute the drag coefficient (CD) for given lift coefficient (CL)
        and Mach number, considering critical Mach number effects.

    _cl(mass, tas, alt, vs=0):
        Compute the lift coefficient (CL) for given aircraft mass,
        true airspeed (TAS), altitude, and vertical speed.

    clean(mass, tas, alt, vs=0):
        Compute the total drag (N) for the aircraft in clean configuration.

methods.name: openap.addon.bada4.Thrust.cT
documentation: Compute the thrust coefficient, considering the altitude, Mach, and rating.

methods.name: openap.addon.bada4.Thrust.climb
documentation:
Compute the thrust force during the climb phase.

Parameters:
    mass (float | ndarray): The mass of the aircraft (kg).
    tas (float | ndarray): True airspeed (kts).
    alt (float | ndarray): Altitude (ft).
    dT (float | ndarray, optional): ISA temperature deviation (K). Default: 0.
Returns:
    float | ndarray: The thrust force during the climb phase in Newtons.

methods.name: openap.addon.bada4.Thrust.crusie
documentation:
Compute the thrust force during the cruise phase.

Parameters:
    mass (float | ndarray): The mass of the aircraft (kg).
    tas (float | ndarray): True airspeed (kts).
    alt (float | ndarray): Altitude (ft).
    dT (float | ndarray, optional): ISA temperature deviation (K). Default: 0.
Returns:
    float | ndarray: The thrust force during the climb phase in Newtons.

methods.name: openap.addon.bada4.Thrust.takeoff
documentation:
Compute the thrust force at takeoff

Parameters:
    mass (float | ndarray): The mass of the aircraft (kg).
    tas (float | ndarray): True airspeed (kts).
    alt (float | ndarray): Altitude (ft). Default: 0.
    dT (float | ndarray, optional): ISA temperature deviation (K). Default: 0.
Returns:
    float | ndarray: The thrust force during the climb phase in Newtons.

Thrust class for computing the thrust of an aircraft using BADA4 models.

This class provides methods to compute the thrust coefficient and thrust force
during different phases of flight such as climb, cruise, and takeoff.

Attributes:
    ac (str): aircraft type (e.g.: A320 or A320-231).
    a_ (list): List of coefficients for thrust computation.
    b_ (dict): Dictionary of flat rating coefficients for different ratings.
    c_ (dict): Dictionary of temperature rating coefficients for different ratings.

Methods:
    cT(mach, h, rating, dT=0):
        Compute the thrust coefficient considering altitude, Mach, and rating.
    climb(mass, tas, alt, dT=0):
        Compute the thrust force during climb phase.
    cruise(mass, tas, alt, dT=0):
        Compute the thrust force during cruise phase.
    takeoff(mass, tas, alt=0, dT=0):
        Compute the thrust force during takeoff phase.

Compute the fuel flow at not-idle conditions.

Args:

Returns:

FuelFlow class to compute the fuel flow of an aircraft using BADA4 models.

Attributes:
    ac (str): aircraft type (e.g.: A320 or A320-231).
    mass_ref (float): Reference mass (kg).
    f_ (list): List of coefficients for fuel flow computation.
    fi_ (list): List of coefficients for idle rating.
    lhv (float): Lower heating value (J/kg).

Methods:
    idle(mass, tas, alt, **kwargs):
        Compute the fuel flow with idle rating.

    enroute(mass, tas, alt, vs=0, **kwargs):
        Compute the fuel flow at not-idle.

modules.name: openap.fuel
documentation: "OpenAP FuelFlow model.

classes.name: openap.fuel.FuelFlow

methods.name: openap.fuel.FuelFlow.__init__
documentation:
Initialize FuelFlow object.

Args:
    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).
    eng (string): Engine type (for example: CFM56-5A3).
        Leave empty to use the default engine specified
        by in the aircraft database.

methods.name: openap.fuel.FuelFlow.at_thrust
documentation:
Compute the fuel flow at a given total thrust.

Args:
    acthr (int or ndarray): The total net thrust of the
        aircraft (unit: N).
    alt (int or ndarray): Aircraft altitude (unit: ft).

Returns:
    float: Fuel flow (unit: kg/s).

methods.name: openap.fuel.FuelFlow.takeoff
documentation:
Compute the fuel flow at takeoff.

The net thrust is first estimated based on the maximum thrust model
and throttle setting. Then FuelFlow.at_thrust() is called to compted
the thrust.

Args:
    tas (int or ndarray): Aircraft true airspeed (unit: kt).
    alt (int or ndarray): Altitude of airport (unit: ft).
        Defaults to sea-level.
    throttle (float or ndarray): The throttle setting, between 0 and 1.
        Defaults to 1, which is at full thrust.

Returns:
    float: Fuel flow (unit: kg/s).

methods.name: openap.fuel.FuelFlow.enroute
documentation:
Compute the fuel flow during climb, cruise, or descent.

The net thrust is first estimated based on the dynamic equation.
Then FuelFlow.at_thrust() is called to compted the thrust. Assuming
no flap deflection and no landing gear extended.

Args:
    mass (int or ndarray): Aircraft mass (unit: kg).
    tas (int or ndarray): Aircraft true airspeed (unit: kt).
    alt (int or ndarray): Aircraft altitude (unit: ft).
    vs (float or ndarray): Vertical rate (unit: ft/min). Default is 0.
    acc (float or ndarray): acceleration (unit: m/s^2). Default is 0.

Returns:
    float: Fuel flow (unit: kg/s).

methods.name: openap.fuel.FuelFlow.plot_model
documentation:
Plot the engine fuel model, or return the pyplot object.

Args:
    plot (bool): Display the plot or return an object.

Returns:
    None or pyplot object.
Fuel flow model based on ICAO emission databank.

modules.name: openap.extra.aero
documentation:
Aero library inspired by @ProfHoekstra/bluesky.

Functions for aeronautics in this module
    - physical quantities always in SI units
    - lat,lon,course and heading in degrees

International Standard Atmosphere
    p,rho,T = atmos(h)    # atmos as function of geopotential altitude h [m]
    a = vsound(h)         # speed of sound [m/s] as function of h[m]
    p = pressure(h)       # calls atmos but returns only pressure [Pa]
    T = temperature(h)    # calculates temperature [K]
    rho = density(h)      # calls atmos but returns only density [kg/m3]

Speed conversion at altitude h[m] in ISA:
    mach = tas2mach(v_tas,h)    # true airspeed (v_tas) to mach number conversion
    v_tas = mach2tas(mach,h)    # true airspeed (v_tas) to mach number conversion
    v_tas = eas2tas(v_eas,h)    # equivalent airspeed to true airspeed, h in [m]
    v_eas = tas2eas(v_tas,h)    # true airspeed to equivalent airspeed, h in [m]
    v_tas = cas2tas(v_cas,h)    # v_cas  to v_tas conversion both m/s, h in [m]
    v_cas = tas2cas(v_tas,h)    # v_tas to v_cas conversion both m/s, h in [m]
    v_cas = mach2cas(mach,h)    # mach to v_cas conversion v_cas in m/s, h in [m]
    mach   = cas2mach(v_cas,h)  # v_cas to mach conversion v_cas in m/s, h in [m]

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.atmos
documentation:
Compute press, density and temperature at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    (float, float, float) or (ndarray, ndarray, ndarray):
        Air pressure (Pa), density (kg/m3), and temperature (K).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.temperature
documentation:
Compute air temperature at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Air temperature (K).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.pressure
documentation:
Compute air pressure at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Air pressure (Pa).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.density
documentation:
Compute air density at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Air density (kg/m3).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.vsound
documentation:
Compute speed of sound at a given altitude.

Args:
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: speed of sound (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.distance
documentation:
Compute distance between two (or two series) of coordinates using
Haversine formula.

Args:
    lat1 (float or ndarray): Starting latitude (in degrees).
    lon1 (float or ndarray): Starting longitude (in degrees).
    lat2 (float or ndarray): Ending latitude (in degrees).
    lon2 (float or ndarray): Ending longitude (in degrees).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters). Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Distance (in meters).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.bearing
documentation:
Compute the bearing between two (or two series) of coordinates.

Args:
    lat1 (float or ndarray): Starting latitude (in degrees).
    lon1 (float or ndarray): Starting longitude (in degrees).
    lat2 (float or ndarray): Ending latitude (in degrees).
    lon2 (float or ndarray): Ending longitude (in degrees).

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Bearing (in degrees). Between 0 and 360.

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.h_isa
documentation:
Compute ISA altitude for a given pressure.

Args:
    p (float or ndarray): Pressure (in Pa).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: altitude (m).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.latlon
documentation:
Get lat/lon given current point, distance and bearing.

Args:
    lat1 (float or ndarray): Starting latitude (in degrees).
    lon1 (float or ndarray): Starting longitude (in degrees).
    d (float or ndarray): distance from point 1 (meters)
    brg (float or ndarray): bearing at point 1 (in degrees)
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (in meters). Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    lat2: Point latitude.
    lon2: Point longitude

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.tas2mach
documentation:
Convert true airspeed to mach number at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (m/s).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: mach number.

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.mach2tas
documentation:
Convert mach number to true airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    mach (float or ndarray): Mach number.
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: True airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.eas2tas
documentation:
Convert equivalent airspeed to true airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_eas (float or ndarray): Equivalent airspeed (m/s).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: True airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.tas2eas
documentation:
Convert true airspeed to equivalent airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (m/s).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Equivalent airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.cas2tas
documentation:
Convert calibrated airspeed to true airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_cas (float or ndarray): Equivalent airspeed (m/s).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: True airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.tas2cas
documentation:
Convert true airspeed to calibrated airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_tas (float or ndarray): True airspeed (m/s).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Calibrated airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.mach2cas
documentation:
Convert mach number to calibrated airspeed at a given altitude.

Args:
    mach (float or ndarray): Mach number.
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Calibrated airspeed (m/s).

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.cas2mach
documentation:
Convert calibrated airspeed to mach number at a given altitude.

Args:
    v_cas (float or ndarray): Calibrated airspeed (m/s).
    h (float or ndarray): Altitude (m).
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float or ndarray: Mach number.

functions.name: openap.extra.aero.crossover_alt
documentation:
Convert the crossover altitude given constant CAS and Mach.

Args:
    v_cas (float or ndarray): Calibrated airspeed (m/s).
    mach (float or ndarray): Mach number.
    dT (float or ndarray): Temperature shift from ISA (in K).  Defaults to 0.

Returns:

SavitzkyGolay Filter

Parameters
----------
window_size : int
    the length of the window. Must be an odd integer number.
order : int
    the order of the polynomial used in the filtering.
    Must be less then `window_size` - 1.
deriv: int
    the order of the derivative to compute (default = 0
    means only smoothing)

functions.name: openap.extra.fuzzy.gaussmf
documentation:
Gaussian fuzzy membership function.

Parameters
----------
x : 1d array or iterable
    Independent variable.
mean : float
    Gaussian parameter for center (mean) value.
sigma : float
    Gaussian parameter for standard deviation.

Returns
-------
y : 1d array
    Gaussian membership function for x.

functions.name: openap.extra.fuzzy.zmf
documentation:
Z-function fuzzy membership generator.

Parameters
----------
x : 1d array
    Independent variable.
a : float
    'ceiling', where the function begins falling from 1.
b : float
    'foot', where the function reattains zero.

Returns
-------
y : 1d array
    Z-function.

Notes
-----
Named such because of its Z-like shape.

functions.name: openap.extra.fuzzy.smf
documentation:
S-function fuzzy membership generator.

Parameters
----------
x : 1d array
    Independent variable.
a : float
    'foot', where the function begins to climb from zero.
b : float
    'ceiling', where the function levels off at 1.

Returns
-------
y : 1d array
    S-function.

Notes
-----
Named such because of its S-like shape.

functions.name: openap.extra.fuzzy.interp_membership
documentation:
Find the degree of membership `u(xx)` for a given value of `x = xx`.

# Parameters

x

: Independent discrete variable vector.

xmf

: Fuzzy membership function for `x`. Same length as `x`.

xx

: Value(s) on universe `x` where the interpolated membership is
  desired.

zero_outside_x

: Defines the behavior if `xx` contains value(s) which are outside the
  universe range as defined by `x`. If `True` (default), all
  extrapolated values will be zero. If `False`, the first or last value
  in `x` will be what is returned to the left or right of the range,
  respectively.

# Returns

xxmf

: Membership function value at `xx`, `u(xx)`. If `xx` is a single
  value, this will be a single value; if it is an array or iterable the
  result will be returned as a NumPy array of like shape.

# Notes

For use in Fuzzy Logic, where an interpolated discrete membership function
u(x) for discrete values of x on the universe of `x` is given. Then,
consider a new value x = xx, which does not correspond to any discrete
values of `x`. This function computes the membership value `u(xx)`
corresponding to the value `xx` using linear interpolation.

functions.name: openap.extra.fuzzy.defuzz
documentation:
Defuzzification of a membership function, returning a defuzzified value
of the function at x, using various defuzzification methods.

Parameters
----------
x : 1d array or iterable, length N
    Independent variable.
mfx : 1d array of iterable, length N
    Fuzzy membership function.
mode : string
    Controls which defuzzification method will be used.
    * 'mom'     : mean of maximum
    * 'som'     : min of maximum
    * 'lom'     : max of maximum

Returns
-------
u : float or int
    Defuzzified result.

Raises
------
- EmptyMembershipError : When the membership function area is empty.
- InconsistentMFDataError : When the length of the 'x' and the fuzzy
    membership function arrays are not equal.

See Also
--------
skfuzzy.defuzzify.centroid, skfuzzy.defuzzify.dcentroid

    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).
    eng (string): Engine type (for example: CFM56-5A3).
        Leave empty to use the default engine specified
        by in the aircraft database.
Base class for fuel flow models.

modules.name: openap.prop
documentation: Retrieve properties of aircraft and engines.

functions.name: openap.prop.available_aircraft
documentation:
Get available aircraft types in OpenAP model.

Returns:
    list of string: aircraft types.

functions.name: openap.prop.aircraft
documentation:
Get details of an aircraft type.

Args:
    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).

Returns:
    dict: Performance parameters related to the aircraft.

functions.name: openap.prop.aircraft_engine_options
documentation:
Get engine options of an aircraft type.

Args:
    ac (string): ICAO aircraft type (for example: A320).

Returns:
    list of string: Engine options.

functions.name: openap.prop.search_engine
documentation:
Search engine by the starting characters.

Args:
    eng (string): Engine type (for example: CFM56-5).

Returns:
    list or None: Matching engine types.

functions.name: openap.prop.engine
documentation:
Get engine parameters.

Args:
    eng (string): Engine type (for example: CFM56-5B6).

Returns:
    dict: Engine parameters.
